front.title: Virtual Realities
front.subtitle: Simulated environments where artificial intelligence governs the laws of physics and human interaction.

back.text:
In science fiction, virtual reality (VR) is often depicted as a total immersion of the senses, where AI systems generate and maintain complex, persistent worlds. These fictional constructs, such as the Matrix or the Metaverse, explore the philosophical boundary between biological perception and digital data. In these narratives, AI serves as the architect, managing every detail from environmental physics to the behavior of non-player characters.

Technologically, these stories anticipated the development of head-mounted displays, haptic feedback, and spatial computing. While modern VR relies on real-time rendering and tracking sensors, sci-fi often envisions direct neural interfaces that bypass the senses entirely. These concepts challenge us to consider the ethics of digital existence and the potential for AI to create realities indistinguishable from our own.
